meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use project management to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for AI builders.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' dilemma, treat your learning like a professional software delivery cycle. By using project management frameworks to document your architectural decisions, feature iterations, and bug resolutions, you transform raw code into a narrative of technical competence that hiring managers crave for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, paralyzed by the 'empty repository' syndrome. Your skills are sharp, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of abandoned tutorials and unlinked commits. The tech industry doesn't care about what you know; it cares about what you can ship, maintain, and document under pressure.

This isn't just about writing code; it’s about engineering a professional identity. By integrating structured project management into your workflow, you move from a student writing scripts to an engineer delivering products. We’re going to use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, turning your next project into a masterclass of professional documentation.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the business logic and user impact. This demonstrates to recruiters that you understand the 'why' behind the 'how'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Create a PRD for a SaaS project management dashboard that tracks sprint velocity and technical debt. Focus on the user flow for a student building a portfolio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a README.md based on the PRD provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a robust project structure that follows industry-standard patterns like MVC or Clean Architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable project structure for a React + Node.js application. Include folders for services, models, and controllers. Ensure it follows modular best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and create empty boilerplate files for the core modules.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Implementation
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to build features incrementally while maintaining high code quality through enforced linting and testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a clean, modular implementation of a task management service that includes CRUD operations and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the task service using best practices for error handling and logging.'

steps.title: Documenting Technical Decisions
steps.description: Create Architecture Decision Records (ADRs). This is what separates junior developers from seniors in the eyes of hiring managers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an ADR for choosing PostgreSQL over MongoDB for a task management application, highlighting scalability and data integrity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a /docs/adr directory and add the ADR file for the database choice.'

steps.title: CI/CD and Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Showcase your DevOps skills by automating the build and deployment process, making your project 'production-ready'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit tests and linting on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create .github/workflows/main.yml with the provided CI configuration.'

pro_tips: Always commit your ADRs alongside your code to show your thought process, not just the final output.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Change Logs' automatically from your Git history to show consistent progress.
Tag your repository with specific architectural patterns (e.g., #CleanArchitecture) to improve searchability for recruiters.
Write a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README to demonstrate self-reflection and growth mindset.
Record a 60-second screen-share demo of the UI and link it in the PR description to bring your code to life.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, share it on LinkedIn with a 3-paragraph post explaining the problem you solved, the technologies used, and the biggest challenge you overcame during development.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow to turn code into a career-defining asset. Stop building in the dark and start building for your future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
